summary: Comprehensive frontend testing of Yandex Taxi 3D marketing landing page. All 51 required data-testids present. Hero renders with correct H1 ('Move to the rhythm of the city.'), LIVE / 2M+ RIDES DAILY badge, primary CTA 'ORDER NOW', secondary CTA 'HOW IT WORKS', and stats 600+ / 3.5min / 4.9★. 6 WebGL <canvas> elements render (1 hero taxi + 4 how-it-works mini scenes + 1 city map). Marquee contains FAST/SAFE/24/7/GLOBAL. All 6 benefit cards, 4 tariff cards (Comfort highlighted yellow), 4 how-it-works steps, 3 map stats, app store CTAs + phone mockup, and full footer (4 columns + 3 socials + YANDEX.TAXI wordmark + 'Get where you're going. Faster.' CTA) all render. Desktop nav links smooth-scroll to correct sections (benefits->1282, tariffs->2984, how->4011, app->7065, ride->0), all target sections in viewport. Mobile 390px viewport: hamburger toggle opens menu with 5 mobile-nav-link-* items + mobile-nav-cta button; clicking mobile-nav-link-tariffs correctly scrolls to tariffs section. Zero console errors, zero runtime exceptions across desktop + mobile.
critical_code_review_comments:
CityMap.jsx and AppDownload.jsx use
to split headings ('Every street.
Every second.', 'Your ride.
One tap away.'). Purely stylistic; not a bug. innerText search across two lines will not match a single-line phrase.

Landing page mounts many React Three Fiber canvases (6 total). Consider guarding heavy scenes with IntersectionObserver / suspend on offscreen sections for lower-end devices — currently the how-it-works steps and city map all instantiate WebGL contexts on load.
No noticeable file-size violations (all landing components inspected are focused single-purpose modules).
test_credentials: N/A - pure marketing landing, no auth
context_for_next_testing_agent: Frontend URL: https://yandex-ride-preview.preview.emergentagent.com. Pure React marketing landing (no backend). All 51 review-request data-testids verified present. 6 WebGL canvases render (hero taxi + 4 how-it-works mini scenes + city map). Desktop smooth-scroll nav and mobile hamburger menu both working. No console errors. Backend testing intentionally skipped (testing_type='frontend only').
